title: Jurisdiction
description: A legal jurisdiction identified by ISO 3166 codes. Succession law varies not just by country but by subdivision — Scotland differs from England and Wales, and US states have radically different rules

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Always uppercase, exactly two letters

subdivision.description: ISO 3166-2 subdivision code identifying a state, province, or territory with its own succession law
subdivision.$comment: ISO 3166-2. Most codes 2-3 chars; some historic codes longer.

legalSystem.description: The legal tradition governing succession in this jurisdiction. Determines which rules apply to inheritance, forced heirship, and estate administration
legalSystem.$comment: common_law: judge-made precedent (England and Wales, most US states, Australia). civil_law: codified statute (France, Germany, Japan, most of continental Europe). mixed: hybrid of common and civil law (Scotland, South Africa, Louisiana). religious: governed by religious law (not further specified). customary: indigenous or tribal customary law. religious_islamic: Islamic Shariah succession rules (faraid). religious_jewish: Jewish halachic succession law. religious_hindu: Hindu succession law (HSA 1956 in India). religious_canon: Canon law of the Catholic or Orthodox Church. plural: multiple legal systems operating in parallel (Malaysia, Pakistan, Israel, Nigeria).

name.description: Human-readable jurisdiction name for display purposes. Not authoritative — the country and subdivision codes are the canonical identifiers
